title: tetragonotheca repanda
intro: Tetragonotheca repanda, also known as the Yellow Bells or Buttercup Bush, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Health benefits of Tetragonotheca repanda include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive disorders, and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its flowers in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
